danger_signals.visual_form: Entity appearance in towns at specific times; planetary positioning via telescope
danger_signals.meaning: Oppressive atmosphere indicator; signals danger and behavioral corruption of NPCs

danger_signals.visual_form: Townspeople running away from player character
danger_signals.meaning: Shadowlord of Cowardice is active in area; population terrorized

danger_signals.visual_form: Townspeople attempting to steal from player
danger_signals.meaning: Shadowlord of Falsehood is active; moral corruption spreading
Shadowlord of Hatred is active; violence and hostility dominant

danger_signals.visual_form: Implied in town layouts and NPC dialogue (not visually detailed in NES port)
danger_signals.meaning: Blackthorn's totalitarian rule; harsh virtue enforcement

danger_signals.visual_form: Sun rise/set cycle visible in overhead view
danger_signals.meaning: Day/night cycle determines NPC availability, Shadowlord activity, moongate functionality

safety_signals.visual_form: Specific architectural sites (Lycaeum, Empath Abbey, Serpent's Hold) containing flames
safety_signals.meaning: Safe zones for Shadowlord confrontation; repository of virtue principles (Truth, Love, Courage)

safety_signals.visual_form: Party members visible in overhead view (Iolo, Shamino, others)
safety_signals.meaning: Allied support network; narrative safety indicator; Warriors of Destiny faction

safety_signals.visual_form: Magical doorway markers appearing at night in eight locations
safety_signals.meaning: Fast travel accessibility; known waypoint safety; escape route availability

safety_signals.visual_form: Sun position in sky during overhead play
safety_signals.meaning: Reduced Shadowlord activity; most NPC availability; safer travel window

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Amulet (defensive artifact); Codex of Ultimate Wisdom (narrative authority)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Sacred Flames (Truth, Love, Courage); Empath Abbey as safe house; Iolo's hut as gathering point

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Blackthorn himself as visual authority figure (implied through NPC dialogue and rule enforcement)
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Corrupted townspeople executing draconian laws; enforcers of virtue through violence
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Eight cities of Britannia under strict governance; town layouts enforcing control; law-enforcement presence

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Three entities representing Falsehood, Hatred, Cowardice; shards of Mondain's Gem of Immortality
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Faulinei (Falsehood), Astaroth (Hatred), Nosfentor (Cowardice) - distinct behavioral manifestations
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Underworld locations; planetary orbit positions trackable via spyglass; presence affects all eight towns

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Authority figure; lost in Underworld; objective of restoration quest
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Keep/castle locations; Empath Abbey (associated refuge); throne symbolism

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Tyranny active - Blackthorn rules; Shadowlords unchecked
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Oppressive town layouts; corrupted NPC behavior (theft, violence, fear); draconian law messaging in dialogue
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs enforce strict virtues brutally; townspeople flee, steal, or attack based on which Shadowlord dominates; reduced NPC cooperation with player

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Shadowlord of Cowardice active in region
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Townspeople visible fleeing from player position
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Population panic; reduced quest availability; NPC avoidance; combat difficulty increases due to fear-driven chaos

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Shadowlord of Falsehood active in region
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Implied through NPC dialogue patterns; dishonesty and theft behaviors visible
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs attempt to steal from player inventory; dialogue becomes unreliable; quests become deceptive or contradictory

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Shadowlord of Hatred active in region
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Townspeople displayed as aggressive toward player
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: NPCs initiate combat; hostile faction tags; violence-driven social breakdown; city becomes dangerous for movement

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Overhead view shows darkened palette; moongates become visible/active at eight locations
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Different NPCs appear/disappear; Shadowlord movement becomes trackable; moongate phase-based teleportation becomes available; alternative travel routes open

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Bright overhead palette; moongates invisible; standard town activity
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Full NPC availability for dialogue/quests; standard travel required; Shadowlord presence less overt

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Dungeon-heavy environment; three gem shards scattered across specific locations; subterranean aesthetic
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Combat-heavy encounters; quest-critical item acquisition; Shadowlord shard recovery mandatory for victory path

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Sacred Flame sites prepared for Shadowlord confrontation
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Three specific locations (Lycaeum, Empath Abbey, Serpent's Hold) displaying flame graphics; shards present in inventory
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Player must lure Shadowlord to flame, destroy shard; victory condition for each of three Shadowlords; restoration of virtue principles visualized

environmental_storytelling.example: NPCs follow time-of-day schedules; their presence/absence in locations at specific times reveals plot details, quest availability, and world state (who is hiding, who is active during oppressive hours)
Sacred Flames housed in distinct structures (Lycaeum, Abbey, Hold); these contrast visually with ordinary town buildings and communicate spiritual/virtue authority

environmental_storytelling.technique: Corrupted NPC behavior as world-state indicator
environmental_storytelling.example: Townspeople's actions (fleeing, stealing, attacking) directly reflect which Shadowlord currently dominates the region; behavior change requires no dialogue, purely visual/mechanical
Scattered gem shards across Underworld dungeons require exploration; visual placement of shards marks critical quest path; deeper dungeons signal higher story stakes

Nighttime moongates enable rapid transit; daylight requires standard travel; this cycle creates natural tension between safety (day) and opportunity (night), reflecting oppressive world where travel itself is monitored
Iolo and Shamino appear at Iolo's hut as safe haven; their visual presence signals the Warriors of Destiny faction exists and opposes Blackthorn
Planetary orbits visible through spyglass directly show Shadowlord positions; this transforms an item into an environmental storytelling mechanic communicating threat distribution

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Townspeople under Shadowlord of Cowardice influence
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Run away from player character; attempt to flee encounters; reduced dialogue availability; visible panic animations

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Townspeople under Shadowlord of Falsehood influence
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Attempt theft from player inventory; provide false/contradictory dialogue; quest instructions become unreliable

entity_behavioral_tells.entity: Townspeople under Shadowlord of Hatred influence
entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Initiate combat with player; hostile stance in towns; refuse cooperation; violence takes precedence over dialogue

Appear at specific times in different towns; directly affect all NPCs in affected region; trackable via planetary positions; require specific defeat sequence (name discovery, shard recovery, flame confrontation)
Follow scheduled locations; provide dialogue and quest information; available for interaction; normal behavioral baseline
Change locations based on daily schedule; some become unavailable; different NPCs appear; dialogue may shift based on night-specific events
Can repel Shadowlords temporarily; amulet effect non-replicable later; visual power display signaling narrative importance
Absence from throne creates power vacuum; Blackthorn assumes rule; locating him becomes primary narrative goal; visual absence communicates political crisis

notes: Ultima V communicates world-state primarily through NPC behavioral shifts tied to Shadowlord presence and time-of-day cycles. The three Shadowlords each manifest a different corruption pattern (fear/flight, theft/dishonesty, violence/hatred), making their regional presence immediately readable through townspeople actions without explicit UI. The game's time system creates a natural tension between safer daytime travel and dangerous nighttime alternatives, reinforcing oppressive atmosphere. Sacred Flame locations serve as visual safe zones and victory points, contrasting with the corrupted majority of Britannia. The Underworld and scattered gem shards provide environmental storytelling that guides narrative progression. NES port would compress these visual systems but maintain core behavioral tells through sprite animation and NPC positioning.
